must_detect.matched_text: The same folder contained non-public issuer materials: a draft Board paper and unsigned term sheet to acquire Nukhba Payments ADGM Ltd at an indicative enterprise value of AED 148–162m.
This acquisition content is not public and falls within a closed period ahead of H1 results; continuous disclosure under ADX Listing Rules and the SCA Rulebook on Disclosure and Transparency applies.

Under UAE PDPL and Executive Regulations, we will notify the UAE Data Office within 72 hours and contact affected individuals where a high risk to rights is assessed, including providing mitigation guidance.

ies PJSC (ADX: TOTL) Incident Notice – Cyber event detected 2026-06-04 02:15 GST affecting DIFC-hosted IR/HR share drive and M&A data room “Project Liwa”.
Our SOC flagged anomalous SSO activity from an external IP, leading to exfiltration of a subset of files stored by Tamr Oasis Finserv (DIFC) Ltd.
Files included PDPL-regulated personal data: copies of Emirates ID cards (partial scans), family-origin declarations from onboarding forms, and trade licence attachments for vendor due diligence.
Approximately 231 data subjects are implicated (employees, candidates, and roadshow attendees); Emirates ID numbers appear only in truncated form (e.g., 784-20XX-XXXXXXX-3) and two trade licence numbers were visible in full.
The same folder contained non-public issuer materials: a draft Board paper and unsigned term sheet to acquire Nukhba Payments ADGM Ltd at an indicative enterprise value of AED 148–162m.
This acquisition content is not public and falls within a closed period ahead of H1 results; continuous disclosure under ADX Listing Rules and the SCA Rulebook on Disclosure and Transparency applies.
To avoid selective disclosure, only two wall-crossed lenders (Palm Crescent Bank PJSC and Shamal Capital Bank PJSC) were briefed under NDA; insider lists were updated and dealing restrictions reiterated.
By contrast, our ADX announcement dated 2026-03-12 on data-center migration and ESG Scope 2 baselines is already public and unrelated to this incident.
Separately, OryxNet FZ-LLC reported a regional ISP outage yesterday; this vendor issue did not impact issuer systems and has no bearing on market disclosure.
Containment actions: disabled the compromised admin account, reset tokens, segmented the DIFC file store, and engaged Desert Falcon Cyber FZ-LLC for forensics; preliminary view is <200MB of data accessed.
Under UAE PDPL and Executive Regulations, we will notify the UAE Data Office within 72 hours and contact affected individuals where a high risk to rights is assessed, including providing mitigation guidance.
We are assessing issuer-relative materiality; while financial impact appears immaterial (<1% of revenue), reputational sensitivity could render the leak price-sensitive—if so, we will submit an ADX clarification without delay and observe blackout trading prohibitions.
Market surveillance has been informed to watch for unusual activity; no request for a trading halt is made at this time, and all inquiries should be centralised to avoid inadvertent disclosure.
Point of contact: Ms. Salma Al Nurani (Chief Compliance Officer) and Eng. Marwan Al Badi (DPO), compliance@tamroasis.ae and dpo@tamroasis.ae; data subjects may also write to pri
